observations.recommendation:
2.12 We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to:
Conduct a regular reconciliation of their balances so that the adjustments made or errors detected either by the Treasury or Accounting Office will be adjusted and posted in their respective books.
Record and adjust immediately in its respective books all reconciling items found to be valid.
Follow-up the request/demand letter from the concerned depository banks copies of the CMs and DMs to determine their validity and effect the necessary adjustments.
Review and analyze the prior years’ reconciling items which causes and origins remained to be unknown up to this date and locate the supporting documents especially of the bank debits and unrecorded issuance of checks to validate the issuance thereof.
Transfer to unappropriated surplus the amounts of issued checks that remained outstanding for more than two (2) years as provided for in Section 97 of PD 1445.
Closely supervise the assigned personnel especially those assigned in the recording of the books of the treasurer and accounting, as well as those who will prepare the BRS. Thoroughly review and scrutinize documents for the proper booking up of the reconciling items.

3.7	We recommend Management to:
Require the employees to refund immediately the unexpended balance of the cash advances and enforce the immediate liquidation of overdue advances by sending demand letters to the concerned otherwise, the Treasurer is advised to withhold or suspend the salaries of the erring accountable officers which had not settle their cash advances as of December 31, 2016 without prejudice to the other sanctions as provided for under the aforementioned regulations.
Direct the Accountant to issue Certification that that previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books before granting cash advances.
Strengthen measures to demand and assure the recovery of prior year cash advances made by officers and employees in and out of service.
Strictly adhere with the provisions of Sec. 89 PD 1445, COA Cir. No. 97-002 and EO No. 298 and other rulings mentioned above.

4.5	We recommend that since these were releases in earlier years, Management should have immediately prepare and submit the needed documents for the liquidation of the PDAF. Remaining balances of the said fund as reflected in the financial statements cast doubt whether the LGU had complied the aforementioned Supreme Court Decision.

5.8 	We are recommending to the Local Chief Executive to:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer as the Accountable Officer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Petty Cash account cashbooks with the Subsidiary Ledgers of the Accounting Office.
Require the employees to refund immediately the unexpended balance of the cash advances and enforce the immediate liquidation of overdue advances, otherwise, the Treasurer is advised to withhold or suspend the salaries of the erring accountable officers which had not settle their cash advances as of December 31, 2016 without prejudice to the other sanctions as provided for under the aforementioned regulations.

6.11	We enjoin the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to require the collector to immediately restitute the unremitted cash balance and unliquidated cash advances; otherwise, cause the withholding of the salary and other emoluments due to the AO;
Require the collectors to turnover their collections within the prescribed period. The Municipal Treasurer shall see to it that every time collections are turned over, all accountable forms whether issued or not must be presented and accounted for. On the other hand, the issuance of accountable forms to the collectors shall be controlled to ensure that no accountable forms are issued when the previous ones were not fully accounted for; and
Ensure strict adherence to rules and regulations, appropriate disciplinary action shall be taken against accountable officers who fail to perform their functions and to comply with existing rules and regulations in order to avoid these circumstances.

7.5	We recommend that the Municipality should implement the provisions of COA Circular 2007-001 and ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of fund transfers.
Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should review all outstanding accounts and record adjustments for fund transfers that actually partake of the nature of donations.
Further, the Municipality should institute action against NGO/PO’s and their officers/organizers that failed to implement the programs and projects funded by the Municipal government pursuant to the provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement covering these transactions.

8.5	In view of the foregoing, we recommend that Management:
To strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 10121 in planning and implementation of the projects, programs and activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office; and
To refrain from using the LDRRMF for purposes other than those authorized under Section 21 of RA 10121 and Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

prepare a budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; and
request for inputs from the DepEd officials/School Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund.

12.6	We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and other Head of Offices concerned to submit the accounts and other financial reports of the Municipality regularly, on every 10th day of the succeeding month.

Likewise, it is also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Budget Officer advise the Local Chief Executive on the documentary requirements needed in the granting of additional cash advances for Confidential Fund.
